steps.title: Calculate the sum of mass times frequency
steps.content: The frequency table shows masses 10, 20, 30, 40 grams with frequencies 12, 6, 8, 9 respectively. For a frequency table, find the sum of all individual masses by multiplying each mass by how many objects have that mass: $$10 \times 12 = 120$$ $$20 \times 6 = 120$$ $$30 \times 8 = 240$$ $$40 \times 9 = 360$$ Summing these products: $120 + 120 + 240 + 360 = 840$

steps.title: Divide by total count to find the mean
steps.content: The total number of objects is $12 + 6 + 8 + 9 = 35$. Therefore: $$\text{mean} = \frac{840}{35} = 24 \text{ grams}$$
